title: 10 best herbal creams for over bleeding during periods

remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins in Witch Hazel help to constrict blood vessels, reducing blood flow and thus alleviating excessive menstrual bleeding.
Astringent Properties: The astringent properties of Witch Hazel help to dry up excess moisture and reduce swelling in the vaginal area, which can contribute to heavy bleeding.

Anti-inflammatory Compounds: Witch Hazel contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate cramps associated with menstruation, which can exacerbate excessive bleeding.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana, also known as American Witch Hazel, creams helps with over bleeding during periods because of its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins present in the plant help to reduce bleeding and promote clotting by constricting blood vessels and reducing inflammation in the uterine lining. The cream also has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infection and promote healing, making it a popular natural remedy for excessive menstrual bleeding and heavy periods. It can be used externally to reduce bleeding and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Hamamelitannin: A type of tannin, hamamelitannin has astringent properties that help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding.
Oleanolic acid: A triterpenoid saponin, oleanolic acid has anti-inflammatory and astringent effects that may help to reduce inflammation and bleeding in the uterine lining.
Tannins (specifically Gallic acid): Tannins, including gallic acid, have astringent properties that help to reduce bleeding by constricting blood vessels and promoting coagulation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of tannins, which have astringent properties that help reduce bleeding.
Buds: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which aid in soothing and protecting the uterine lining.
Barks: Employed for their tannin content, which helps to reduce bleeding and prevent infections.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried hamamelis virginiana leaves in 2 cups of carrier oil such as jojoba oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Melt 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler and set aside for 10 minutes.
Mix the infused oil with 1/4 cup of shea butter and 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil in a separate bowl.
Combine the melted beeswax with the oil mixture and stir until fully incorporated and thickened.

remedy_properties: Astringent and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Pot marigold cream's astringent and anti-inflammatory properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing excessive bleeding and inflammation associated with heavy menstrual flow.

Antioxidant and Free Radical Scavenging Properties: The antioxidants in pot marigold cream neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress, which may exacerbate heavy bleeding during periods, helping to protect the uterine lining from damage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with over bleeding during periods because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's flavonoids and saponins help to reduce swelling and ease cramping, while its antimicrobial properties prevent infection and promote wound healing. Additionally, Calendula's ability to stimulate blood clotting and reduce menstrual flow makes it an effective natural remedy for excessive bleeding during periods, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a sense of calm.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoid Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help to reduce bleeding by constricting blood vessels and preventing fluid loss.

Flavonoids (especially Quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid found in Calendula, has anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant properties, which can help to reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms of menorrhagia.
Phenolic Acids (especially Caffeic Acid): Caffeic acid has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms of menorrhagia.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers contain triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to reduce bleeding and promote healing.
Leaves: The leaves have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and protect the skin, reducing bleeding and discomfort during menstruation.
Seeds: The seeds contain triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to reduce bleeding and promote healing in the uterine lining.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 calendula flowers when fully open and dry them in a dark place for 2 weeks to preserve their potency.
Infuse 1 cup of dried calendula flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 70-80 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1-2 tablespoons of coconut oil to create a smooth consistency.
Whip the mixture until light and fluffy then pour it into jars and store them in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.

remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Stinging nettle creams contain astringent compounds that help constrict blood vessels, reducing blood flow and minimizing bleeding.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Nettle's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling in the uterus, which can contribute to heavy menstrual bleeding.
Vasoprotective properties: Nettle's vasoprotective compounds help strengthen and stabilize blood vessel walls, preventing them from rupturing or leaking excessively during menstruation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with over bleeding during periods because of its rich content of iron and other essential nutrients. The cream is believed to help in regulating menstrual flow, reducing inflammation and soothing the uterine lining, thereby reducing excessive bleeding. The anti-inflammatory properties present in the cream also help in calming the uterine muscles, promoting a more balanced menstrual cycle. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh urtica dioica leaves and flowers from a clean and unpolluted area.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces and dry them in a warm and well-ventilated area for 1-3 days.
Mix 1 cup of dried urtica dioica with 1 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20-30 minutes until it reaches a temperature of 180-190°f and thickens.
Strain the hot mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and let it cool completely.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of tilia platyphyllos leaves and flowers on a sunny day in july.
Dry the collected plant material in a warm room for 2 weeks at 30c.
Infuse 250g of dried tilia platyphyllos in 1000ml of cold-pressed oil for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and mix it with 100g of beeswax and 50g of candelilla wax.
Heat the mixture at 40c for 10 minutes to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_recipe: Weigh 200 grams of dried vaccinium macrocarpon berries and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine 100 grams of beeswax with 200 grams of coconut oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Add the vaccinium macrocarpon powder to the warm oil mixture and stir until well combined for 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in 20 grams of shea butter and 10 grams of vitamin e oil.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify for at least 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Weigh 250g of ginkgo biloba extract standardized to contain 24% flavone glycosides and 6% terpene lactones.
Combine 10g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Mix 20g of shea butter and 10g of vitamin e oil with the melted beeswax mixture.
Add 2.5ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir in 2.5g of triethanolamine.

Combine 1 cup of dried taraxacum officinale roots and leaves with 1 cup of carrier oil in a saucepan.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried chamomile flowers and measure out 2 tablespoons.

Steep the mixture for 4 hours at room temperature then strain it through cheesecloth.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax pellets with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and warm them together gently.
Combine the cooled chamomile infusion with the wax-oil mixture and whip until smooth.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers for one cup quantity and clean them properly.
Combine the cleaned herbs with carrier oil like coconut or sweet almond oil in a ratio of one part herb to two parts oil.
Heat the mixture at low temperature around 150-160 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes to 1 hour to extract the active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids then add vitamin e oil as a preservative in a dosage of five drops per ounce.
Transfer the extracted herbal infusion into clean glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for a minimum period of 2 weeks to allow the mixture to mature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh fennel seeds, clean them thoroughly, and grind into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the ground fennel powder with 100ml of coconut oil, 20ml of beeswax, and 10ml of vitamin e oil in a heat-resistant bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler and gently heat it over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 30 minutes until melted and well combined.
Remove the mixture from the heat, let it cool to room temperature, then whip it using an electric mixer or whisk for 5 minutes until creamy and light.
Transfer the cream to sterilized glass jars, label them with the date and ingredients, and store in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature below 40°f.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia absinthium leaves and dry them immediately using a food dehydrator or air drying method.
Combine 50 grams of dried artemisia absinthium leaves with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to allow for optimal herbal extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 50 grams of beeswax and 20 milliliters of vitamin e oil in a double boiler, heating gently until melted and smooth.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried melissa officinalis leaves from a trusted source or purchase them online for use in the recipe.
Grind 20g of dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered melissa officinalis with 100ml of sweet almond oil and 50ml of beeswax pellets in a double boiler setup for 10 minutes.
Heat the mixture at 60°c to 70°c until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture has a smooth consistency taking approximately 15 minutes.
Remove from heat let cool slightly then pour into sterilized containers and store in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gardenia jasminoides flowers and leaves in the morning to ensure optimal potency for infusion.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours.
Combine 20g of dried gardenia jasminoides with 100ml of cold pressed coconut oil in a double boiler and heat gently for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and let it cool to room temperature before refrigerating for 2 hours.
Whip 50g of unflavored yogurt with 10 drops of vitamin e oil and add the cooled infused oil mixture, whipping until smooth and creamy for application.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves.
Combine the harvested material with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture for four weeks by storing it in a cool dark place, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid from the solids through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container discarding the solids and reserving 50 milliliters of the infused oil.
Mix the reserved infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and heat gently in a double boiler until melted and combined.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and dry them in shade for two weeks.
Grind the dried herbs into fine powder using mortar and pestle, then sift through a mesh strainer.
Mix 20 grams of the herb powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler, heating it gently for one hour.
Remove from heat and let it infuse for four hours before filtering the mixture through cheesecloth or cotton cloth into a clean glass jar.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter to the filtered liquid, melting them in a double boiler while stirring constantly for five minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers on a sunny morning.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm oven at 60°c for 2 hours.
Mix 20 grams of dried thymus vulgaris with 40 grams of sweet almond oil and heat it in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and stir until melted, then remove from heat and let cool for 30 minutes.
Whip the mixture using an electric mixer for 5 minutes or until it reaches a smooth creamy consistency.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for over bleeding during periods are topical treatments made from plant extracts that help to reduce excessive menstrual bleeding. These creams work by constricting blood vessels and reducing inflammation, which can help to slow down heavy bleeding. Examples of herbal creams that help with over bleeding during periods include those containing aloe vera, witch hazel, and chamomile. By using these creams, women can experience significant relief from heavy bleeding and improve their overall quality of life, reducing the need for medical interventions and allowing them to engage in daily activities with more confidence.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to over bleeding during periods that are treated with herbal creams are hemorrhoids, varicose veins, and eczema. These conditions can cause discomfort, itching, and inflammation, making them ideal candidates for natural treatments. Herbal creams containing ingredients like witch hazel, aloe vera, and tea tree oil can soothe and calm the affected areas, reducing pain and discomfort while promoting healing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with over bleeding during periods is a blend of Calendula and Witch Hazel creams. Calendula cream is known for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help reduce bleeding and pain. Witch Hazel cream, on the other hand, has astringent properties that can help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding. Applying a combination of these creams can provide relief from heavy bleeding and discomfort associated with menstrual periods. They can also help soothe and calm the vaginal area.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Archives of Gynecology and Obstetrics, herbal creams can be beneficial for over-bleeding during periods due to their anti-inflammatory and antiproliferative properties. The study found that 23 medicinal plants, belonging to 18 families, exhibited anti-inflammatory activity in vitro and/or in vivo, which can help in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of over-bleeding. Specifically, plants such as Cuscuta chinensis and Portulaca oleracea exhibited estrogenic activity, and others like Boswellia carteri and Punica granatum showed inhibitory activity on biosynthesis of prostaglandins, which can contribute to reduced menstrual bleeding.
